commit.message:
feat: add post-resolve validate and release v1.5.0

Validate
- Implement `validate` as a Proc-based runtime option.
- Run `validate` after all parameters finish resolving, coercing, transforming, and writing into `px`.
- Execute `validate` in the current controller context so callbacks can read `px` and call controller methods such as `current_user`.
- Short-circuit the post-validation pass when an endpoint tree contains no `validate` callbacks.

Schema DSL
- Make `type: { ... }` build nested object schemas.
- Support object-level defaults for `type: { ... }`.
- Make `type: []` equivalent to bare `[]`.
- Keep `type: Hash` equivalent to `type: Object`.

Docs And Tests
- Sync the English and Chinese README for nested object timing, whitelist extraction, `type` keyword semantics, and the new `validate` option.
- Add regression coverage for nested object execution order, custom validation timing, `type: { ... }`, `type: []`, and object defaults.
- Align CI and gem metadata with the Ruby 3.1 support baseline.

Verification
- Run `bundle exec rspec`.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(feed): use canonical category-based product URLs in feeds (#876)

Google Merchant and OpenAI feeds were generating incorrect product URLs.
GMC hardcoded /products/{slug} which 301-redirects (penalized by Google).
OpenAI feed incorrectly included merchant slug in URL path and used raw
category text instead of proper slugs.

Both feeds now use buildProductUrl() from seo-utils.ts which generates
the canonical /{category-slug}/{product-slug} pattern. The OpenAI feed
also resolves custom domains via buildMerchantBaseUrl() instead of
deriving URLs from the request host header.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement menubar popover panel

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Work (#6)

* Revamp dashboard, clients and appointments UI; add Conversations & Automations pages; reorganize settings/system/sidebar

### Motivation
- Simplify and refocus several admin screens to surface operational actions and reduce clutter from deep analytics and rarely-used filters.
- Provide compact, action-oriented summaries and visualizations so teams can quickly spot issues (AI handoffs, cancellations, errors) and act.
- Introduce two new working screens for monitoring dialog flow (`conversations`) and automation scenarios (`automations`).
- Reorganize navigation and settings to separate core MVP actions from legacy/secondary functionality.

### Description
- Reworked the dashboard (`app/page.tsx`) to show AI-centric KPIs and small charts rendered with `recharts`, added automation summary cards, and simplified recent-activity rendering; replaced several legacy charts/components with compact area/bar charts and attention cards.
- Rebuilt the appointments view (`app/appointments/page.tsx`) to surface operational status cards, a status bar chart, and an inline simplified table instead of the previous full-featured table and funnel/heatmap visualization.
- Simplified the clients screen (`app/clients/page.tsx`) by removing a large set of advanced filters and campaign UI, replacing them with lightweight actionable filters, summary metric cards, and a concise client table with scenario tags and next-appointment info.
- Added `app/conversations/page.tsx` to monitor inbound dialog flow, channel distribution and AI vs human handling with chart and list views.
- Added `app/automations/page.tsx` to present scenario metrics, health indicators and simple controls for automation scenarios.
- Updated system and settings pages (`app/system/page.tsx`, `app/settings/page.tsx`) to reorganize sections, add anchors/IDs for intra-page navigation, and surface core controls (agent toggle, knowledge uploads, roles, notifications) with clearer labels.
- Updated the sidebar (`components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`) to reflect new navigation structure, introduce a small "Future / Legacy" section for less-used links, and tune item rendering behavior for anchor/hash links.
- Misc: removed several complex helper utilities and UI modules from these pages, introduced `formatDate`/`formatCurrency` usages, adjusted icons and color palettes, and added small UX text/content changes across pages.

### Testing
- Performed a local type-check with `tsc --noEmit`, which completed successfully.
- Built the application with `yarn build` and verified the production build step completed without errors.
- Ran linting (`yarn lint`) and unit test command (`yarn test`) where available; these commands completed without reported failures.

* Restore data-driven admin screens and navigation

* Adjust settings page layout

* Move auto-systems section to settings

* Add period selector to dashboard revenue chart

* Refine revenue chart range and period pickers

* Switch revenue date picker to date-based options

* Limit revenue date options by registration and show data guard

* Show only used date options in revenue picker

* Restrict revenue picker to current date options

* Rename revenue block title on dashboard

* Fix dashboard revenue source and resilient appointments load

* Make dashboard metric cards more compact

* Align compact KPI values to bottom-right

* Increase compact KPI value size and offsets

* Reduce compact KPI card footprint

* Adjust KPI title/description colors and badge size

* Use Unbounded font for metric card titles

* Replace analytics monthly revenue chart with period money movement chart

* Show revenue chart when at least one point is available

* Fix RevenueChart container height so chart renders

* Match Analytics KPI card design to Dashboard metric cards

* Move analytics communication volume banner to page header section

* Place communication volume banner to the right of period selector

* Rename analytics trends title to use slash separator

* Match communication banner backing style to period selector

* Reduce trends header backing height to match period block

* Make analytics top control blocks use equal backing height

* Add full analytics period selector with contextual date options

* Restrict analytics date options to periods intersecting registration date

* Hide past dates from analytics period date selector

* Show registration-start label in date block for all-time period

* Use real org registration date source for analytics period labels

* Reduce compact metric value offsets from right and bottom

* Prevent services card from stretching to chart row height

* Raise recent activity block and lower appointments block for aligned bottoms

* Restructure homepage columns to move recent activity and appointments up

* Restore revenue block height and move appointments row upward

* Lift recent activity card upward toward services block

* Fix dashboard card overlap in right column

* Stack dashboard right column blocks with consistent spacing

* Prevent revenue chart card from stretching vertically

* Extend daily appointments block to align with activity block

* Update page.tsx

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Work (#110)

* Make weekly leaderboard a live hosted weekly-best competition

### Motivation
- Convert the existing weekly leaderboard from a multi-run local-fallback model into a hosted-only live weekly competition that ranks players by their best single score for the current UTC week.
- Prevent local fallbacks from showing stale or offline data in the hosted leaderboard card and ensure consistent server-side validation and tie-break rules.

### Description
- Database: removed use of `counted_runs`, added a reinforced index and an RPC `upsert_weekly_best_leaderboard_entry(...)` which inserts-or-updates with `total_score = greatest(existing_best, submitted_score)` and uses `created_at ASC` as a stable tie-break for equal scores; added migration `supabase/migrations/202603251100_weekly_best_live_competition.sql` and updated `supabase/weekly_leaderboard.sql` accordingly.
- Edge Function: updated `supabase/functions/upsert-leaderboard-entry/index.ts` to validate a single non-negative integer `total_score`, keep trusted `player_id` from the JWT and server-side handle lookup, and call the new RPC so lower scores do not overwrite a higher weekly best; returned value reports the stored weekly best.
- Frontend: removed the local leaderboard fallback for the hosted card, hid the leaderboard card when offline, when Supabase config is missing, or when the hosted fetch fails, and added lightweight polling for live refresh on the weekly page with proper cleanup when leaving the page; updated UI copy to reflect the weekly-best competition and show rank, handle, best score and league lines.
- Docs and agent snapshot: updated `README.md` and `AGENTS.md` to document the weekly competition rules, the best-single-score ranking, and the hosted-only leaderboard card behaviour.
- Files changed include `supabase/weekly_leaderboard.sql`, `supabase/migrations/202603251100_weekly_best_live_competition.sql`, `supabase/functions/upsert-leaderboard-entry/index.ts`, `app.js`, `index.html`, `README.md`, and `AGENTS.md`.
- Migration notes: run `supabase db push` or apply the new migration file; the migration drops `counted_runs` and creates the RPC used by the Edge Function; re-deploy the `upsert-leaderboard-entry` Edge Function after the migration so it uses the new RPC.

### Testing
- Static site validation: ran `bash ./scripts/validate-static-site.sh` which passed.
- JavaScript sanity check: ran `node --check app.js` which completed without syntax errors.
- Edge Function type check: attempted `deno check supabase/functions/upsert-leaderboard-entry/index.ts` but `deno` is not available in this environment, so server-side type checking should be run in CI or locally with Deno; function logic was updated to call the new RPC and remaining behaviour is covered by the migration.
- Note: runtime verification against a Supabase instance (apply migration, deploy Edge Function, exercise writes and live reads) is recommended in staging to confirm RLS, service role grant and live polling behaviour.

* Simplify weekly page to hosted global leaderboard only

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: add scope compaction and schemas bang control

Scope
- Add `compact` and `compact_blank` options to `scope`.
- Apply these options to the custom `px.scope[:name]` bucket after validation.
- Keep the behavior shallow and leave top-level `px` plus built-in scope buckets unchanged.

Schemas
- Add `bang:` to `.schemas` with a default of `true`.
- Keep bang keys such as `name!` by default.
- When `bang: false`, emit plain keys and write requiredness as `required: true`.
- Keep `only:` compatible with bang-style names even when bang output is disabled.

Docs And Tests
- Sync the English and Chinese README examples and explanations.
- Add regression coverage for scope options and `.schemas(bang: false)`.

Verification
- Run `bundle exec rspec`.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: add px transforms and release v1.4.0

Implementation
- Implement `transform`, allowing a Symbol or Proc to customize values after coercion.
- Implement `px` / `px_key` to customize the key name written into `px`.
- Keep transformed and renamed values consistent across nested schemas, scopes, and headers.
- Release these changes as version 1.4.0.

Docs
- Fix README rendering where config details were not breaking cleanly in rendered Markdown.
- Move the `tag` explanation into the `doc` section instead of keeping a separate heading.
- Rename "DSL Reference" / "DSL 详细说明" to `doc`-scoped DSL wording and add the level-4 headings to the top table of contents.
- Change the English table of contents to a numbered list to match the Chinese README.
- Keep the English and Chinese READMEs in sync.

Verification
- Run `bundle exec rspec`.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(codex): detect skills from exec_command args, filter internal tools

Codex has no skill invocation events. Skills are loaded by reading
SKILL.md via exec_command (sed, cat, python). This fix:

- Filters internal tools (exec_command, spawn_agent, write_stdin)
- Parses exec_command arguments for skills/{name}/SKILL.md pattern
- Deduplicates by session (multiple reads = 1 invocation)

commit.message:
feat: refine request body and required contracts

User Requests
- Fix the materialize_missing keyword mismatch behind the "parameter declaration and invocation do not match" report.
- Make required mean non-nil while still allowing blank values.
- Remove allow_nil.
- Allow blank as shorthand for allow_blank.
- Support required: true when not using bang syntax.
- Implement body! runtime validation.
- Remove the resp alias.

Documentation Sync
- Expand the body/json/form note: same-media-type declarations merge, multiple media types are emitted in OpenAPI, and runtime field validation/coercion still reads from Rails params.
- Sync the revised Chinese README wording into the English README.
- Note that OpenAPI-facing options such as default are omitted when they cannot be converted into YAML, for example a proc default.
- Use Chinese wording in the Chinese I18n examples.

Implementation
- Unify field construction so bang syntax and required: true share the same required semantics.
- Track root request body requiredness through the request contract and emit it in runtime validation and OpenAPI generation.
- Tighten scalar casting and missing handling so invalid values do not leak into px.
- Add regression coverage for nil vs blank semantics, non-bang required fields, root body validation, removed aliases, and OpenAPI output.

Verification
- Run bundle exec rspec.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Force fork-based PR creation (#2)

Always push generated update branches to the authenticated user's fork, open PRs from fork-qualified heads, and include the PinActions repository link plus the exact CLI invocation in generated PR bodies. Fix fork polling and redirected-fork detection for live runs, document the fork-based workflow in the README, and record the classic-token requirement discovered during smoke testing.

Co-authored-by: DilumAluthge <5619885+DilumAluthge@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): make blueprint checkdecls ignore comparator-only spec files

The blueprint workflow's declaration check imports every lean_lib in the
workspace. That is incompatible with `BridgelandSpec`, which intentionally
restates theorem names for comparator use and collides with the actual
BridgelandStability declarations.

- add a local `checkdecls` executable that skips the `BridgelandSpec` library
  when importing workspace roots
- export the `BridgelandBlueprint` wrapper declarations under the module system
  so `checkdecls` can still see the blueprint nodes

Verified with:
- lake exe cache get
- lake build BridgelandStability
- lake exe runLinter
- lake build BridgelandBlueprint:blueprint
- lake exe checkdecls blueprint/lean_decls

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix browser tab flickering, sticky tab logic, download defaults, keep-alive

Browser detection:
- Sticky tab logic — locks onto a tab, only switches when a new tab appears
  or the locked tab is closed. No more flickering between tabs.
- Keep-alive: locked tab stays marked as playing on every poll
- MediaRemote polling disabled when it returns empty (no error spam)
- Fallback to browser scanning only when MediaRemote is blocked

Downloads:
- Artwork saves directly to ~/Downloads/ by default (no file picker)
- Configurable download path in Settings → General → Downloads
- Folder picker button shows current path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): make blueprint theorem text LaTeX-safe

Rewrite the LeanArchitect blueprint statement/proof text in
`BridgelandBlueprint.Main` as actual LaTeX instead of markdown-style prose with
unicode symbols in text mode.

This fixes the GitHub Actions blueprint job, which was failing during the PDF
build on theorem text such as `Σ ⊂ Stab(D)` and `Hom_ℤ(..., ℂ)`.

Verified with:
- lake exe cache get
- lake build BridgelandStability
- lake exe runLinter
- lake build BridgelandBlueprint:blueprint
- cd blueprint/src && latexmk -output-directory=../print

Summary:

- make the docs link easier to find from README and Marketplace content

commit.message:
fix(storefront): use headers() routing mode for /products redirect (#868)

* fix(web): eliminate dynamic calls from storefront pages for PPR compatibility

Remove headers(), cookies(), and draftMode() calls from storefront page
components and generateMetadata functions so Next.js 16 can generate static
shells with Dynamic IO (cacheComponents: true). This eliminates ~50+/hr
"Expected the resume data" errors in production.

Key changes:
- New buildStoreUrl() utility replaces all headers()-based URL construction
- Two-tier proxy URL normalization replaces per-page lowercase redirects
- Dead cookie reads removed from 5 pages (wallet, imei-check, repairs, swap, category)
- Theme cookie reads removed from layout + homepage (V2ThemeProvider handles client-side)
- Blog post page wrapped in Suspense; canonical URL fix for doubled-slug bug
- Cart cookies() moved inside Suspense boundary
- Blog listing + pages/blog switched to getPublicSupabaseClient()
- Layout keeps minimal headers() for routing mode (consistently dynamic, no PPR errors)
- .avif added atomically to STATIC_FILES_REGEX, IMAGE_FILES_REGEX, config.matcher
- CodeRabbit review fixes: dead vars, vi globals scoped to test overrides,
  afterEach cleanup, notFound assertion, unused slug prop, full-page fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): address CodeRabbit review findings on PPR branch

- Pass postUrl to BlogPostBody to prevent doubled-slug URLs on subdomains
- Add error handling for cart VAT settings query
- Fix domain-identifier fallback in buildStoreUrl for custom domain slugs
- Expand layout theme comment explaining PPR trade-off
- Add NEXT_PUBLIC_ROOT_DOMAIN test for buildStoreUrl
- Add BlogPostPageFallback snapshot test
- Delegate pages/blog to getCachedBlogListing instead of raw Supabase query
- Extract duplicated getSeoData helper in category page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): address reviewer follow-up findings

- Use toBeInTheDocument() instead of toBeDefined() in BlogPostPageFallback test
- Add inline comment documenting NODE_ENV redirect limitation for Vercel preview
- Add Blog + BreadcrumbList JSON-LD structured data to blog listing page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): use headers()-based routing mode for /products redirect

Replace NODE_ENV check with proxy header detection for the /products/[slug]
category redirect. NODE_ENV=development incorrectly assumed path-mode routing,
breaking Vercel preview where NODE_ENV=production but routing is path-based.

The /products route is explicitly force-dynamic since it mostly returns 308
redirects — PPR offers near-zero benefit for redirect-only responses.

For [category]/[productSlug], keep NODE_ENV check with documented limitation
to preserve PPR on the common render path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): remove force-dynamic incompatible with cacheComponents

Route segment config `dynamic` is not allowed when `nextConfig.cacheComponents`
is enabled. The `headers()` call already opts this route into dynamic rendering
automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): unify product schema offers and breadcrumb URLs via getProductUrl (#871)

Previously, offers.url used a hardcoded /products/ path while breadcrumbs
used the category-based path from getProductUrl(). This sent conflicting
canonical signals to search engines. Now both use getProductUrl() for
consistent SEO signals.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(aug): correct degenerate `translate_percent` range in AUG_AGGRESSIVE (#863)

(0.1, 0.1) is a degenerate range that forces Albumentations Affine to
always shift right/down by exactly 10%; never left or up. Corrected to
(-0.1, 0.1) for symmetric bidirectional translation, consistent with the
signed companion parameters rotate: (-15, 15) and shear: (-5, 5).

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[glyf] Reject simple glyphs with too few points

Reject simple glyf data that claims fewer points than contours.
Such data cannot describe a valid outline, so fail before
allocating and decoding contour points.

Testing: CCACHE_DISABLE=1 meson test -C b
Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add shared websocket protocol layer and ingress validation

Define a zod-backed websocket contract for handshakes, message envelopes, operations, and runtime parsing.

Split wire and normalized BoardSubscriptionCompleted handling, validate raw socket payloads at router ingress, and expose the contract through the new public subpath export microboard/protocol.

Add focused router/contract tests plus consumer guidance for UI and server agents.

Behavior note: valid messages should be unchanged, but malformed socket payloads are now rejected at ingress instead of reaching handlers.

commit.message:
扩展 GitHub 与 GitLab skill 为平台级 CLI 交互 (#34)

## 背景
- 现有 GitHub / GitLab skill 的名称和描述过于聚焦 issue、PR、MR，容易把触发范围限制死。
- 实际使用中还会涉及更广泛的平台交互场景，例如 GitLab wiki。
- 同时不希望在 AGENTS.md 中直接耦合具体 skill 名称，应更多依赖 skill 自身的 name 和 description
做判断。
- 另外，当前模型在执行 `git add`、`git commit`、`git push` 时容易倾向并行调用，实际会因为
`.git/index.lock` 导致部分 Git 命令失败，需要在 `git-commit` skill 中补充明确约束。

## 变更
- 将 `github-pr-issue` 重命名为 `github-cli`，将 `gitlab-mr-issue` 重命名为
`gitlab-cli`。
- 调整两个 skill 的 description 和正文说明，从 issue / PR / MR 场景扩展到平台级 CLI 交互场景。
- 在 `gitlab-cli` 中补充 wiki 场景，明确优先检查 `glab` 子命令，不足时使用 `glab api`。
- 清理 `AGENTS.md` 中对具体 skill 的直接引用，仅保留平台交互应优先使用官方 CLI 或 API、且尽量非交互的原则。
- 更新 README 中的 skill 列表说明，和新的 skill 名称与定位保持一致。
- 在 `git-commit` 中新增 Git 写操作默认串行执行的约束，明确避免并行触发多个 `git add`、`git
commit`、`git push`，并补充 `index.lock` 相关处理说明与顺序执行示例。

## 验证
- 未运行自动化测试；本次仅涉及文档与 skill 定义调整。

---------

numerical Grothendieck group and use instance synthesis in the numerical
complex-manifold theorem. Also drop the false finite-generation dependency
from the Corollary 1.3 local-homeomorphism proposition-object and sync the
README to the current signatures and componentwise manifold statement.

refactor: make the stability-condition API class-map-first

Promote `PreStabilityCondition.WithClassMap` and `StabilityCondition.WithClassMap`
to the primary generic API for class maps `v : K₀ C →+ Λ`, and re-express the
numerical layer as the canonical specialization to the Euler numerical quotient.

- define the `WithClassMap` topology and generic local-homeomorphism package
- keep `ClassMapStabilityCondition` only as the comparison subtype for the
  surjective factorization bridge
- make `NumericalStabilityCondition` the `WithClassMap` specialization to
  `numericalQuotientMap`
- update the manifold layer to expose public theorems on `WithClassMap`
- rename the old heart-equivalence prestability helper to `PhasePackage` and
  add the reusable `Slicing.ext` lemma used by the deformation proofs
- sync the README and audit artifacts to the new architecture
- keep the paper-facing Theorem 1.2 proposition-object as an explicit wrapper
  over the generic class-map-first layer

commit.message:
Add knip and remove unreachable internal code

Add a repo-local knip configuration and package script focused on file and dependency hygiene for the published library entrypoints.

Use the initial knip pass to remove internal files that are no longer referenced anywhere in the codebase, including empty barrels, fully commented-out RichText rendering code, unused transformer helpers, stale spatial-index utilities, and old tooling scripts.

Also remove two leftover unused imports so the default knip run stays clean without broad export-level noise.

Verification: bun run knip; bun test src/Events/Log/createEventsList.test.ts

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix YouTube scrobbling: proper covers, artist parsing, multi-tab, restart state

YouTube improvements:
- Fetch Last.fm album art instead of video thumbnails for YouTube music
- Also fetches album name from track.getInfo for proper scrobble metadata
- Falls back to YouTube thumbnail only when Last.fm has no art
- Parse "Track - Artist" reversed titles by validating against Last.fm artist pages
- UGC videos parse artist from title, not channel name
- Strip (Official Video), [MV], (Audio) etc. from titles
- Multi-tab detection: scans ALL music tabs, picks newest changed one

Album detail:
- Filter out tiny/placeholder artwork URLs (34s, 64s, default star)
- Save artwork button (current cover → NSSavePanel)
- Save All button (all covers → folder picker, numbered files)

Scrobble timing:
- On app restart, seed accumulated time from track's elapsed position
- Progress bar and scrobble ring start at correct position, not zero
- Auto-scrobble if already past threshold on restart

API tracking wired into all 6 services via URLSession.trackedData()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
[Android] Add new BpkSearchInputControl component (#2626)

Add BpkSearchInputControl component with docking support

Introduce BpkSearchInputControl — a non-editable search field with
prefix support (None, Text, Icon), focus state, clear action, and
docking (Float/Top/Middle/Bottom) for stacking controls with shared
corner rounding. Available in OnDefault and OnContrast styles.

- Extract BpkStaticFieldImpl for Select and SearchInputSummary-ReadOnly
- Extract shared field utilities into BpkFieldImplShared
- Focused border: 2dp blue with white gap; no border when unfocused
- Revert BpkSearchInputSummary to original API (no Docking)
- Add stories, snapshot tests, and README
